title: brain index repair — --json success output
description: Converge the retrieval index to consistency without a full rebuild. Drives the reconciliation pipeline (retrieval-index-contract.md §3) only for the notes `index verify` reports as stale or divergent: re-chunk → re-embed → write → verify-complete → CAS activate → retire → mark. Intrinsically idempotent and projection-only: a no-op when the index is already consistent, and each executed run appends exactly one run.projection git-ref audit event. Never touches the ledger tables, the vault, or canonical git refs. The response is a single, unambiguous partial-repair outcome: `outcome` is `converged` when every targeted note was repaired, or `partial` when one or more notes could not converge — in which case `unresolved[]` is required and each entry carries typed failure details (code + retryable + message). Per-note permanent/retryable failures are surfaced ONLY as `unresolved[]` data on this success envelope, never as the command-level error envelope; the error envelope is reserved for whole-command failures that prevent the run from executing at all (bad config, missing store/index, lock contention, internal fault).

outcome.description: `converged`: every targeted note was repaired (or nothing needed repair). `partial`: at least one note could not converge — `unresolved[]` is then required and non-empty. This is the single source of truth for partial repair; it is never also signalled through the command-level error envelope.

repaired.description: One entry per note converged; empty when nothing needed repair (a clean no-op with outcome=converged).

action.description: The convergence action taken for this note (§3).

generationId.description: The generation now active for the note after repair (its active_generation_id, §2).

unresolved.description: Notes that could not be converged this run. Required and non-empty iff outcome=partial; absent when outcome=converged. Each entry is a typed failure detail, never a silent drop — the aggregate exit code is derived from these entries' `retryable` flags (see x-atlas-contract.exitPrecedence).

code.description: `embedding-retryable`: a provider-retryable ProviderError (rate_limit/quota/timeout/transport) — re-running repair may converge it. `embedding-failed`: a non-retryable ProviderError (authentication/model_incompatible/embedding-dimension mismatch) — needs operator action.

retryable.description: True iff re-running the command may converge this note without operator intervention. MUST be true for code=embedding-retryable and false for code=embedding-failed (enforced by the per-item conditionals).

retryAfterMs.description: Provider-directed retry delay in integer milliseconds; present iff the provider supplied timing (a normalized rate_limit/quota retryAfter).

unresolved.message: provider rate limited the embed batch

x-atlas-contract.summary: Converge stale/divergent notes via the reconciliation pipeline (§3); projection-only, idempotent; per-note failures surface as unresolved[] data (one partial-repair outcome).
sideEffects: re-embeds and re-activates stale/divergent notes and retires orphaned generations
emits exactly one run.projection git-ref audit event (executed projection-only command)
prohibitedEffects: never touches ledger tables or db_schema_migrations
a crash mid-repair must converge on rerun with no duplicate chunks or orphaned active generation
a per-note repair failure is NEVER reported through the command-level error envelope — only as an unresolved[] entry

outcomeProtocol.note: Partial repair is signalled exactly once, via outcome=partial + unresolved[]. The command-level error envelope is reserved for whole-command failures (config/db/index/lock/internal) that prevent execution.
exitPrecedence: 7 partial with every unresolved entry retryable (provider-retryable; caller may re-run)
4 partial with any non-retryable unresolved entry (permanent; needs operator action)

errorCodes.when: the SQLite store file is missing or unopenable
the LanceDB index is not configured/present
the exclusive vault-maintenance lock is held by a live process
unexpected internal failure that aborts the whole command before it can report an outcome

aggregateExitCodes.when: outcome=partial and every unresolved entry is retryable (all provider-retryable); mirrored per-note in unresolved[].code
outcome=partial and at least one unresolved entry is non-retryable; mirrored per-note in unresolved[].code
